item.title: `rustc_public` has incomplete implementations
item.body:
rustc_public (nightly-2026-04-03) has incomplete implementations:

```rust
impl<'tcx> Stable<'tcx> for ty::Pattern<'tcx> {
    type T = crate::ty::Pattern;

    fn stable<'cx>(
        &self,
        tables: &mut Tables<'cx, BridgeTys>,
        cx: &CompilerCtxt<'cx, BridgeTys>,
    ) -> Self::T {
        match **self {
            ty::PatternKind::Range { start, end } => crate::ty::Pattern::Range {
                // FIXME(SMIR): update data structures to not have an Option here anymore
                start: Some(start.stable(tables, cx)),
                end: Some(end.stable(tables, cx)),
                include_end: true,
            },
            ty::PatternKind::NotNull => todo!(),
            ty::PatternKind::Or(_) => todo!(), // 👈 this panics
        }
    }
}
```

```rust
thread 'rustc' (210999) panicked at /rustc-dev/55e86c996809902e8bbad512cfb4d2c18be446d9/compiler/rustc_public/src/unstable/convert/stable/ty.rs:491:39:
not yet implemented

error: could not compile `getrandom` (lib)

but the latest rustc_public seems [good](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/blob/3c00c96d3af4d5b5e101e56cc161a608b21366ee/compiler/rustc_public/src/unstable/convert/stable/ty.rs#L526-L528).

And unfortunately, rustc_public crate has bugs as the following error is weird:

```
error: internal compiler error: /rustc-dev/55e86c996809902e8bbad512cfb4d2c18be446d9/compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/normalize_erasing_regions.rs:171:13: Failed
